raw_text:
Do not, under any circumstances, open an account with Marcus… In March of 2025, I opened an account with Marcus.
After no more than a weeks time, my account was locked, and soon after that, my account was closed completely. They say it’s because I was in violation of one of their policies, and that is their right, and I will not waste time arguing the minutia of what they saw versus what actually happened, but I’m not happy with the fact that they never told me which policy was violated or how I violated the policy. I never got a warning that they were going to close or lock the account. I woke up one morning, logged onto the account, only to find the account was frozen.
As of now, they still have the money that I deposited to open the account in the first place. I’ve placed numerous calls to them, asking/demanding/complaining for my money to be returned to me and as of this writing, I still don’t have my money back. They keep insisting that my money is “perfectly safe with them”. My opinion is that the money isn’t perfectly safe with them because it’s not perfectly safe with me.
To me, this almost seems like theft. They took my money, close the account, and now they’re refusing to give my money back, or at the very least, they’re taking their own sweet time sending the money back. This is mid-May now, so we’re talking about two months or more since the account was closed. That, in my opinion, is a totally unacceptable situation. 
If this can happen to me, it could happen to you as well. Stay away from Marcus at all costs.

raw_text:
Couldn’t get my money for a house closing! I had been a Marcus customer since September 2022 but recently closed my account due to a frustrating experience that left me unable to access my own money when I needed it most. After initiating the transfer for the P&S, I received an alarming call instructing me to contact Marcus. The first agent I spoke with had no idea why I was called, and I was transferred SEVEN times, each time having to re-authenticate with three pieces of information. The agents repeatedly cited ‘quick money rules,’ despite the fact that I had more than five times the necessary amount in my account for over two years, and a significant deposit had been made four months prior.

Eventually, one agent was able to assist, but even she couldn’t assure me that I would be able to wire the funds for my closing. Given this uncertainty, I transferred my entire balance to another bank, which could issue a certified check. This process, however, was equally painful—I spoke with 11 different representatives before finally being able to move my money.

While I valued the high interest rates and ease of use with Marcus, what’s the point of a high-yield savings account if you can’t access your funds when you need them most? This experience ultimately led me to close my account.

raw_text: This bank is a literal SCAM TLDR: My experience with Marcus savings has truly been the epitome of shady, incompetent online banking. I'm not someone to write bad reviews, but this has been such a frustrating and ridiculous situation, and I want to warn everyone, please, DO NOT trust this bank. It's one thing if an account is closed and customer service isn't able to provide an explanation behind this, but shouldn't getting the funds back be made an absolute priority? I'm not sure why this bank has deliberately made this become such a complicated and stressful process, where even after 4 YEARS they still have refused to give my money back. I had an account opened with Marcus back in 2020, which suddenly got closed a few months later. When I tried calling customer service, they refused to give me any explanation behind the account's closure. After several attempts to call them regarding getting the money back into my original bank account, I still never received my funds. In July, I had a family emergency that required me to travel for a few weeks - prior to leaving, I desperately tried to call back to see if my money could be sent back to me before I left. They said that a check would be mailed to me within 14 business days and to my surprise, there was no check in the mail once I got back. Again, I tried to call them regarding this, and they stated that their so-called 'review team' would be reopening my account within 30 business days instead. I called again today (which is when I should have received an update regarding the reopening of my account) and yet again, still there is no update regarding if or when I'll be able to get my money back. Endless attempts to speak with both customer service and specialists have been failures, as they are entirely clueless about any updates from the 'review team', and now I haven't been given any timeframe as to when I'll be receiving an update at all. It's rather repulsing to see that a bank with such a good reputation and reviews, being "rated #1 in customer service" can be this negligent and incapable of addressing basic matters.

Neither of the competitors mentioned above have done the shenanigans Marcus did.

Avoid Marcus and Goldman like the plague.

This feature was incredibly convenient for getting a quick snapshot of my total financial status without having to manually sum up individual account balances.

Poor MFA Support Chase is one of the largest banks in the world, yet its app-to-website MFA experience feels like it was designed by teams that have never spoken to each other—or tested their own product.

Absolute dogwater of an app EDIT:

Bank of America’s fraud system is so bad and prone for false positives that it’s like they don’t want me as a customer or something. They are making it extremely hard to find reasons to want to stay as a customer. I’m very frustrated and it’s only been 1 month into using this card.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:
App is SOOO annoyingly cluttered. Every single time I open the app I get some or other pop-up about an annoying disclaimer, promotion, or confirmation of contact information. It’s like their team designed the app just to make the legal team happy instead of making it for users trying to do simple everyday financial tasks in an easy and quick way.

Also trying to connect an account via their direct account connection straight up does not work.

I’m only using this app because they’re the bank provider for my airline card. But based on the app, I would not consider banking with them. I’m also a Citibank and Chase user and, among the big banks, Chase has the best app by far.

I don’t understand how they have such a bad app in this day and age when well designed apps are so common. Meanwhile, fintechs are gaining market share because they actually understand how important this is.

raw_text:
Terrible App Design, but the Bank Itself Is Good The Bank of America app has one of the worst user experience designs I’ve seen, and it has stayed that way for years without any real fixes.

Every time you try to send money, you have to remember and type the exact amount because the app doesn’t show your statement balance, minimum balance, or total available balance. You’re forced to switch between screens and keep the number in your head.

Entered amount but after changed destination? Enter it again because the app erases it.

You can’t copy any numbers anywhere. I just want to view a transaction and copy its amount if needed, but the app doesn’t allow that. So I have to remember another number here.

Copied somewhere else, going to paste, no way! Not all fields work to paste from clipboard, you have to type manually. So many mistakes done because of the pure UX.

The whole app feels like a web page pretending to be an app. It’s not a real native iOS experience, just a slow web wrapper that looks and feels outdated.

To be fair, the bank’s service itself is good. The app is what ruins the experience.
If they ever fix the app properly, I’ll come back and update this review.

Extremely Frustrating and Unprofessional Customer Service I recently received a replacement Citi Costco Visa card. After activating it, I tried to add it to my Apple Pay — but it simply wouldn’t go through. I contacted Citi customer service three separate times, speaking with three different representatives, and none of them were able to resolve the issue.

Each call dragged on with long hold times and unnecessary “security checks,” even after I provided all requested information multiple times. Despite more than an hour combined on the phone, the problem is still unresolved. What’s most frustrating is that I never had this issue with my previous card, so something clearly changed on Citi’s end.

As a loyal Costco member for nearly 40 years with an excellent 850 credit score, I expected a much higher level of professionalism and technical competence. Instead, I experienced confusion, lack of training, and poor communication from every representative I spoke with.

Citibank needs to seriously review and improve its customer service training. Representatives should be knowledgeable, courteous, and able to handle basic issues promptly. This was one of the most disappointing customer service experiences I’ve ever had — enough to make me reconsider using this card altogether.

raw_text:
Find another credit card company I’ve had a really disappointing experience. I never received any alerts about a payment issue until two days after it failed — and it was barely over $100. Then, three days later, I find out the payment was returned with no notice and I was charged both a return fee and an interest fee.

I check my cards daily, and there were no app notifications or emails until five days past the due date saying my payment was late. That’s unacceptable. To make things right, I paid off the full balance immediately — over $1,300 — yet when I contacted support to request a courtesy waiver for the fees, I was told they couldn’t do anything.

That’s too bad, because I have a $9,700 purchase coming up this week, and it’ll be going on my other daily-use credit card (you know, the one from A–Z). They give the same 2% cashback and better service.

This card is only really good for gas cash back at Costco, but with all the options out there now, even that’s not enough to justify the hassle. Chase treats customers better.

raw_text:
Frustrating Experience with Citibank Customer Service I typically don’t have issues with Citibank—until I actually need help with something important. When I recently needed assistance, I encountered a frustrating experience. There seemed to be a language barrier with the staff, and rather than working to understand and resolve my issue, they were mostly silent and unhelpful.

Instead of addressing the problem, I was redirected back to the same customer service number I originally called, forcing me to start the entire process over again. It felt like the staff was more trained in saying they can’t help than actually working toward a solution. This cycle of being passed around with no real answers felt designed to frustrate you into giving up.

I’m sharing this review so others are aware of what they might face when seeking support from Citibank. Hopefully, it encourages the company to improve the effectiveness of its customer service team.
